falsificationAttempts.targetDescription: Hybrid phased migration is lower correctness risk than full rewrites
falsificationQueries: workflow deterministic-change hazards during phased code evolution
evidence that in-flight workflows can fail across orchestration code changes
falsificationAttempts.falsificationAnalysis: Phased migration keeps old and new orchestration paths live simultaneously while code changes continue. Workflow docs and maintainer guidance indicate active workflows are sensitive to step-graph changes and can fail on determinism violations. That is a direct correctness risk introduced by prolonged hybrid overlap, weakening the claim of lower risk by default.

falsificationAttempts.targetDescription: Hybrid migration will deliver material throughput gains (e.g., 2x) before correctness risk rises
falsificationQueries: convex scheduled and action concurrency ceilings
limits that cap gains independent of scan optimization
falsificationAttempts.falsificationAnalysis: Convex imposes hard ceilings on scheduled dispatch and concurrent scheduled jobs; actions also have concurrency caps. These limits can become dominant once scan hotspots are reduced, so 2x throughput is not a robust expectation without explicit quota headroom analysis. Throughput upside is plausible but not established by the hypothesis.
